The post Trader Says Ethereum Competitor That’s Up 140% in Three...

data.title: Andre Cronje announced Sonic’s four visions for the next 25 years, including fee subsidies, dynamic handling fees, etc.
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News: Andre Cronje posted on the X platform that Sonic's vision for this year is quite simple:
· Fee monetization (90% of the fees will go to the application, not the validator and MEV)
· Fee subsidies (eliminating the fee barrier for users to enter the market)
· Dynamic fees (applications can set fees for users)
· Native economy and account abstraction (no wallet or fees required)
The above mainly involves two points:
· Make it as easy as possible for users to try out applications launched on Sonic (remove all barriers to entry and use)
· Help our application developers earn a lot of money

data.title: Sanctum staking is now live
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Sanctum published a statement on the X platform stating that staking has been launched, and 30 million CLOUD will be distributed as active staking rewards (ASR) to users participating in staking and governance.

data.title: Pundi AI and Monad reach technical integration to promote decentralized data annotation
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Pundi AI announced that it will integrate its decentralized data platform with the Monad testnet, further promoting the democratization of artificial intelligence development by combining Monad's high-performance blockchain infrastructure. The integration will enable data contributors to record their annotation work on the chain in the form of NFTs, ensuring transparency and traceability. 
As a blockchain platform capable of processing thousands of transactions with low latency and low cost, Monad will provide an ideal foundation for Pundi AI's "annotate and earn" ecosystem. Pundi AI founder Zac Cheah said that the cooperation with Monad will accelerate the goal of making artificial intelligence development open to everyone. With the upcoming launch of the Monad mainnet, the two parties will continue to expand the platform's functionality to create more opportunities for contributors and developers.

data.title: Significant Buy Interest Observed At Key Bitcoin Price Level
data.description: According to BlockBeats, on-chain data from February 21 indicates that 2.76 million addresses collectively acquired 2.1 million BTC at an average price of $97,100. This suggests a notable level of buying interest at this price point. Should the market experience further downward pressure, this area could serve as a strong support level.

data.title: BitFuFu acquires a Bitcoin mining data center at $400,000 per megawatt, totaling $20.4 million
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News BitFuFu announced the acquisition of a 51-megawatt operating Bitcoin mining data center in Oklahoma, further expanding its mining layout in the United States. The transaction was completed at a price of US$400,000 per megawatt, and the transaction funds came from BitFuFu's cash and common stock. The data center has been in operation since 2022, with more than 95% uptime and an electricity cost of approximately US$0.03 per kilowatt-hour, making the cost of Bitcoin production at this location as low as US$18,000 (excluding mining machines and facility depreciation). BitFuFu plans to deploy AntMiner S21 series mining machines at the site, which is expected to increase its overall computing power scale and support its global 1GW expansion plan. (GlobeNewswire)

data.title: Gary Cardone dumps his XRP position at $2.71, doesn’t think XRP army will get rich
data.description:
Gary Cardone, a seasoned investor and twin brother of entrepreneur Grant Cardone, announced he sold his entire XRP position at $2.71. Posting his today on X, Cardone, drawing from his 40 years of financial experience, cited six personal investment rules as the driving force behind his exit. In his post, Cardone told XRP investors he […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/gary-cardone-dumps-his-xrp-position-at-2-71/
